    Examples¶

    Let's have a look at the different PlantUML diagrams that the cwl2puml APIs are able to generate, given, for example, the app-water-bodies.0.3.0 Workflow.

    1. Parsing¶

    It is needed to read first a CWL document from a remote public URL:

    In [1]:
    Copied! 
    import cwl_loader

cwl_document = cwl_loader.load_cwl_from_location('https://github.com/eoap/zarr-cloud-native-format/releases/download/0.3.0/app-water-bodies.0.3.0.cwl')
    import cwl_loader cwl_document = cwl_loader.load_cwl_from_location('https://github.com/eoap/zarr-cloud-native-format/releases/download/0.3.0/app-water-bodies.0.3.0.cwl')

    2. PlantUML diagrams¶

    The samples below show how to render CWL document to a specific PlantUML diagram:

    In [2]:
    Copied! 
    from cwl2puml import (
    to_puml,
    DiagramType
)
from io import (
    StringIO,
    BytesIO
)
from IPython.display import display_png
from plantuml import deflate_and_encode
from PIL import Image
from urllib.request import urlopen

def render(
    diagram_type: DiagramType,
    debug: bool = False
):
    out = StringIO()
    to_puml(
        cwl_document=cwl_document,
        diagram_type=diagram_type,
        output_stream=out
    )

    clear_output = out.getvalue()

    if debug:
        print(clear_output)

    encoded = deflate_and_encode(clear_output)
    diagram_url = f"https://www.plantuml.com/plantuml/png/{encoded}"

    with urlopen(diagram_url) as url:
        img = Image.open(BytesIO(url.read()))
    display_png(img)
    from cwl2puml import ( to_puml, DiagramType ) from io import ( StringIO, BytesIO ) from IPython.display import display_png from plantuml import deflate_and_encode from PIL import Image from urllib.request import urlopen def render( diagram_type: DiagramType, debug: bool = False ): out = StringIO() to_puml( cwl_document=cwl_document, diagram_type=diagram_type, output_stream=out ) clear_output = out.getvalue() if debug: print(clear_output) encoded = deflate_and_encode(clear_output) diagram_url = f"https://www.plantuml.com/plantuml/png/{encoded}" with urlopen(diagram_url) as url: img = Image.open(BytesIO(url.read())) display_png(img)

    2.1 Component diagram¶

    The component diagram represents the CWL document like chips on a motherboard.

    In [3]:
    Copied! 
    render(DiagramType.COMPONENT)
    render(DiagramType.COMPONENT)

    2.2 Class diagram¶

    The class diagram represents the CWL document like specifications of the previously illustrated chips on a motherboard.

    In [4]:
    Copied! 
    render(DiagramType.CLASS)
    render(DiagramType.CLASS)

    2.3 Sequence diagram¶

    The sequence diagram represents how the previously illustrated chips on a motherboard are triggered and interact with each other.

    In [5]:
    Copied! 
    render(DiagramType.SEQUENCE)
    render(DiagramType.SEQUENCE)

    2.4 State diagram¶

    The state diagram represents the dependencies between the inputs/outputs of the previously illustrated chips on a motherboard.

    In [6]:
    Copied! 
    render(DiagramType.STATE)
    render(DiagramType.STATE)
